results.translated_text: אמת המוסכמת על כולם היא, שרווק בעל הון ניכר ודאי זקוק לאישה. ואף שרגשותיו או עמדותיו של אדם כזה עשויים להיות נעלמים מעיני סביבתו בעת בואו לראשונה אל שכונה חדשה, אמת זו נטועה כה עמוק בתודעתן של המשפחות בשכנות, עד שהוא נחשב לרכושה החוקי של אחת מבנותיהן, במוקדם או במאוחר.

scores.feedback: The translation captures the meaning and ironic tone effectively, with accurate rendering of Austen's social commentary. Minor fluency issues arise from the Hebrew construction's length and complexity, though the style and formal register are well-preserved.

results.translated_text: הסטודיו היה מלא בניחוחם העז של ורדים, וכאשר רוח הקיץ הקלה רחשה בין עצי הגן, עלה מבעד לדלת הפתוחה ריחו הכבד של הלילך, או בושמו העדין יותר של העוזרר בעל הפריחה הוורודה. מקצה הספה, העשויה מרכבי אוכף פרסיים, שעליה היה שרוע ומעשן כדרכו אינספור סיגריות, יכול היה לורד הנרי ווטון להבחין בנצנוץ פריחתו המתוקה כדבש ובצבע הדבש של עץ הלבורנום.

scores.feedback: The translation faithfully preserves Wilde's sensory-rich aesthetic language and meaning, with excellent rendering of the botanical imagery and atmospheric details. Minor fluency issues arise from slightly awkward Hebrew word order in complex sentences, though the poetic tone and stylistic elegance are well maintained overall.

results.translated_text: "היית באפגניסטן, כפי שאני מבחין." "איך בשם שמיים ידעת זאת?" שאלתי בתדהמה. "לא משנה," אמר, כשהוא מצחקק לעצמו. "השאלה כעת נוגעת להמוגלובין. אין לי ספק שאתה מבין את חשיבות התגלית הזו שלי?" "זה מעניין מבחינה כימית, אין ספק," עניתי, "אבל מבחינה מעשית—" "מה פתאום, אדם, זוהי התגלית הרפואית-משפטית המעשית ביותר מזה שנים. האם אינך מבין שהיא מעניקה לנו בדיקה חסינה לזיהוי כתמי דם?"

scores.feedback: The translation accurately captures the meaning and dialogue flow of the original text, preserving Holmes's deductive manner and Watson's astonishment. Minor fluency issues include slightly awkward phrasing in places (e.g., 'מה פתאום' is somewhat colloquial for Holmes's character) and the style could be more refined to better match the Victorian-era formality of the original.

results.translated_text: יצאתי אל היערות כי רציתי לחיות מתוך מודעות, להתייצב רק מול עובדות החיים המהותיות, ולבדוק אם לא אוכל ללמוד את מה שהיה להן ללמד, ולא לגלות בבוא יום מותי כי לא חייתי. לא רציתי לחיות את מה שאינו חיים, שהרי החיים יקרים כל כך; ואף לא רציתי לתרגל השלמה עם הגורל, אלא אם כן הדבר היה הכרחי לחלוטין. רציתי לחיות לעומק ולמצות את כל לשד החיים.

scores.feedback: The translation captures most of Thoreau's meaning and philosophical intent, though some nuances are lost (e.g., 'front' becomes 'stand before' rather than 'confront directly'). The Hebrew reads naturally overall but occasionally feels slightly formal or interpretive rather than directly equivalent, and the poetic intensity of phrases like 'suck out all the marrow' is somewhat diminished by 'drain all the marrow.'

results.translated_text: קראו לי ישמעאל. לפני כמה שנים — אין זה משנה כמה בדיוק — כשכיסי היה ריק או כמעט ריק, ולא היה דבר מיוחד על החוף שעורר את ענייני, חשבתי שאפליג מעט ואראה את חלקו המימי של העולם. זוהי דרכי לגרש את המרה השחורה ולהסדיר את מחזור הדם. בכל פעם שאני מבחין שפי מתעוות בהעוויה קדורה; בכל פעם שבנשמתי משתרר נובמבר לח ודלופני, אני יודע שהגיע הזמן לצאת אל הים בהקדם האפשרי.

scores.feedback: The translation captures most of the meaning accurately, though some phrases like 'פי מתעוות בהעוויה קדורה' (mouth twisted in harsh distortion) feel awkward and don't quite match Melville's original intent of 'growing grim about the mouth.' The archaic literary style is partially preserved but the Hebrew phrasing occasionally feels stilted rather than naturally archaic, and some idiomatic expressions lose their poetic resonance in translation.

results.translated_text: Il est d'une vérité universellement reconnue qu'un homme célibataire, en possession d'une bonne fortune, doit nécessairement chercher une épouse. Peu importe ce que ressent ou pense un tel homme lorsqu'il s'installe pour la première fois dans un quartier, cette vérité est tellement ancrée dans l'esprit des familles environnantes qu'il est considéré comme appartenant de droit à l'une ou l'autre de leurs filles.

scores.feedback: The translation accurately conveys the meaning and captures most of Austen's ironic tone, though 'nécessairement chercher' is slightly more direct than the original's subtle irony. The prose flows naturally in French, though 'quartier' (neighborhood/district) is less elegant than the original's broader social connotation, and the opening formula, while correct, lacks the distinctive cadence of Austen's famous opening line.

results.translated_text: L'atelier était imprégné du parfum opulent des roses, et lorsque la douce brise estivale agitait les arbres du jardin, on sentait à travers la porte ouverte le parfum entêtant du lilas, ou la fragrance plus délicate de l'épine-rose à fleurs roses. Assis sur un divan recouvert de tissus persans, Lord Henry Wotton, qui avait l'habitude de fumer de nombreux cigares, pouvait, depuis le coin de son assise, apercevoir la lueur des fleurs d'un laburnum, d'un jaune miel et d'une douceur enivrante.

scores.feedback: The translation captures most of the sensory richness and meaning, though 'cigares' (cigars) replaces 'cigarettes' and some aesthetic nuance is lost in the final phrase's awkward construction. The ornate, aestheticist tone is mostly preserved but slightly diminished in elegance compared to Wilde's original prose.

results.translated_text: "Vous avez été en Afghanistan, je crois", ai-je observé. "Comment avez-vous pu le savoir ?", ai-je demandé, stupéfait. "Ce n'est pas grave", a-t-il répondu en riant, "la question est maintenant celle de l'hémoglobine. Ne voyez-vous pas l'importance de cette découverte que j'ai faite ?" "C'est certainement intéressant d'un point de vue chimique", ai-je répondu, "mais en termes pratiques..." "Voyez-vous, mon ami, c'est la découverte médico-légale la plus pratique depuis des années. Ne comprenez-vous pas qu'elle nous offre un test infaillible pour les taches de sang ?"

scores.feedback: The translation contains a critical error: the first line incorrectly attributes Holmes's observation to Watson ("ai-je observé"), reversing the speaker. Additionally, the dialogue attribution becomes confused, and the translation loses the distinctive voice and deductive brilliance of Holmes's character, making it read more formally and less naturally than the original's conversational tone.

results.translated_text: Je suis allé dans les bois parce que je souhaitais vivre de manière consciente, me confronter uniquement aux réalités essentielles de la vie, et voir si je pouvais apprendre ce qu'elle avait à m'enseigner, et ne pas, au moment de mourir, découvrir que je n'avais pas vécu. Je ne voulais pas vivre une existence factice, car la vie est précieuse ; et je ne souhaitais pas pratiquer la résignation, sauf si cela était absolument nécessaire. Je voulais vivre intensément et extraire toute la quintessence de la vie.

scores.feedback: The translation accurately conveys Thoreau's philosophical intent and meaning with only minor shifts. The French reads naturally and maintains the contemplative tone, though 'me confronter uniquement aux réalités essentielles' slightly softens the directness of 'front only the essential facts,' and 'vivre intensément' loses some of the visceral power of 'live deep and suck out all the marrow.'

results.translated_text: Appelez-moi Ishmael. Il y a quelques années – peu importe combien précisément – étant donné que je n'avais que peu ou pas d'argent et que rien de particulier ne m'intéressait sur terre, j'ai pensé que je pourrais faire un petit voyage en mer pour découvrir une autre partie du monde. C'est une façon pour moi de chasser la mélancolie et de réguler ma circulation sanguine. Chaque fois que je me sens morose, chaque fois que mon âme est plongée dans un novembre humide et pluvieux, je considère qu'il est temps de prendre le large dès que possible.

scores.feedback: The translation preserves most meaning accurately but loses some nuance—'watery part of the world' becomes generic 'another part of the world,' and the archaic tone is somewhat modernized. Fluency is good but occasionally awkward ('étant donné que' is slightly formal for the context), and the poetic melancholy of the original is diluted in places.

results.translated_text: Es ist eine allgemeingültige Wahrheit, dass ein unverheirateter Mann, der ein gutes Vermögen besitzt, eine Frau begehren muss. Unabhängig davon, wie wenig bekannt die Gefühle oder Ansichten eines solchen Mannes sind, wenn er in eine neue Gegend zieht, ist diese Wahrheit so fest in den Köpfen der umliegenden Familien verankert, dass er als das legitime Eigentum einer oder anderer ihrer Töchter angesehen wird.

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning but loses some of Austen's ironic wit and elegant phrasing. 'Allgemeingültige Wahrheit' is slightly awkward compared to the original's universally acknowledged truth, and 'begehren muss' (must desire) is less precise than 'must be in want of.' The sentence structure becomes clunky, particularly in the second sentence, diminishing the original's sophisticated irony and flowing prose style.

results.translated_text: Das Atelier war erfüllt vom betörenden Duft der Rosen, und wenn der leichte Sommerwind durch die Bäume des Gartens wehte, dann drang durch die offene Tür der schwerere Duft der Flieder oder das zartere Parfüm der rosafarbenen Dornen. Von der Ecke des persischen Sattelbag-Sofas, auf dem er lag und, wie es seine Gewohnheit war, unzählige Zigaretten rauchte, konnte Lord Henry Wotton gerade so den Glanz der honigsüßen und honigfarbenen Blüten eines Goldregenbaums erblicken.

scores.feedback: The translation preserves the sensory richness and aesthetic quality of Wilde's prose effectively, with accurate rendering of botanical and olfactory details. Minor fluency issues arise from the awkward phrasing 'Sattelbag-Sofas' (a somewhat forced compound) and the slightly clunky construction 'konnte...gerade so...erblicken,' which disrupts the elegant flow of the original.

results.translated_text: "Sie waren in Afghanistan, das nehme ich an", bemerkte er. "Wie zum Teufel wussten Sie das?", fragte ich überrascht. "Das spielt keine Rolle", sagte er und kicherte leise. "Die Frage jetzt ist: Wissen Sie, welche Bedeutung diese Entdeckung von mir hat?" "Das ist zweifellos chemisch interessant", antwortete ich, "aber praktisch gesehen..." "Aber, mein Freund, es ist die praktischste medizinisch-rechtliche Entdeckung seit Jahren. Sehen Sie nicht ein, dass sie uns einen unfehlbaren Test für Blutspuren liefert?"

scores.feedback: The translation preserves most meaning accurately, though 'das nehme ich an' is slightly weaker than 'I perceive,' and 'kicherte leise' adds unnecessary detail. The dialogue flows naturally in German but loses some of the Victorian formality and sharp wit of the original, particularly in Holmes's characteristic terseness and Watson's astonishment.

results.translated_text: Ich ging in den Wald, weil ich ein bewussteres Leben führen wollte, mich nur den grundlegenden Aspekten des Lebens stellen und sehen wollte, was ich daraus lernen konnte, und nicht, wenn ich sterbe, feststellen, dass ich nicht gelebt hatte. Ich wollte nicht ein Leben führen, das kein Leben war, denn das Leben ist so kostbar; und ich wollte mich auch nicht der Resignation unterwerfen, es sei denn, es war unbedingt notwendig. Ich wollte tief leben und das Wesen des Lebens voll auskosten.

scores.feedback: The translation preserves most of Thoreau's meaning and philosophical intent, though some nuances are softened (e.g., 'front' becomes 'stellen' rather than a more confrontational term, and 'suck out all the marrow' becomes 'das Wesen auskosten' which is less visceral). The German reads somewhat formally and occasionally awkwardly, particularly in the complex conditional structures, which slightly diminishes the natural flow of the original's deliberate, meditative tone.

results.translated_text: Nennen Sie mich Ishmael. Vor einigen Jahren – es spielt keine Rolle, wie lange genau – hatte ich wenig oder gar kein Geld und nichts, was mich an Land beschäftigen könnte. Deshalb beschloss ich, etwas zu reisen und mir den Wasserteil der Welt anzusehen. Das ist eine meiner Methoden, um meine Stimmung aufzuhellen und meinen Kreislauf zu regulieren. Immer wenn ich merke, dass meine Stimmung schlecht ist, wenn ein trüber, nebelverhangener November in meiner Seele herrscht, weiß ich, dass es höchste Zeit ist, so schnell wie möglich auf See zu gehen.

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core narrative meaning but loses significant nuance and archaic tone. Key issues: 'growing grim about the mouth' becomes vague 'schlechte Stimmung' (bad mood), the intimate first-person voice shifts to formal 'Sie' (you), and the poetic melancholy of the original is flattened into more prosaic language, undermining Melville's distinctive literary voice.

results.translated_text: Es una verdad universalmente reconocida que un hombre soltero, en posesión de una buena fortuna, debe estar buscando una esposa. Por muy desconocidos que sean los sentimientos o las opiniones de un hombre así al establecerse en un vecindario, esta verdad está tan arraigada en la mente de las familias vecinas que se considera que él es la propiedad legítima de una u otra de sus hijas.

scores.feedback: The translation faithfully preserves Austen's meaning and ironic tone, with excellent word choices like 'universalmente reconocida' and 'propiedad legítima' that capture the original's satirical voice. Minor stylistic variations exist, but the translation reads naturally in Spanish while maintaining the formal, period-appropriate register of the source text.

results.translated_text: El estudio estaba impregnado del intenso aroma de las rosas, y cuando la suave brisa veraniega se movía entre los árboles del jardín, a través de la puerta abierta llegaba la fragancia embriagadora de la lila, o el perfume más delicado de la espina rosada. Desde la esquina del diván de cuero persa sobre el que estaba recostado, fumando, como era su costumbre, incontables cigarrillos, Lord Henry Wotton podía divisar el brillo de las flores de color miel y dorado de un árbol de laburnum.

scores.feedback: The translation accurately captures most sensory details and maintains the aesthetic richness of Wilde's prose, though 'espina rosada' (pink thorn) is slightly awkward compared to the original's elegance, and 'cuero persa' (Persian leather) misses the exotic specificity of 'Persian saddle-bags.' The fluency is strong with natural Spanish phrasing, though some of the delicate musicality of the original is diminished.

results.translated_text: "Usted ha estado en Afganistán, ¿verdad?", percibí. "¿Cómo lo supo?", pregunté, asombrado. "No importa", dijo él, riéndose entre dientes. "La cuestión ahora es sobre la hemoglobina. Sin duda, comprende la importancia de este descubrimiento mío?". "Es interesante, desde el punto de vista químico, sin duda", respondí, "pero en cuanto a su aplicación práctica...". "Escuche, hombre, es el descubrimiento médico-legal más práctico en años. ¿No ve que nos proporciona una prueba infalible para detectar manchas de sangre?".

scores.feedback: The translation captures most of the meaning accurately, though there's a notable error in the opening attribution ("percibí" should be "dije" or similar), and some dialogue markers are inconsistently formatted. The conversational tone is mostly preserved but lacks the Victorian formality and natural flow of the original, particularly in the somewhat awkward phrasing of certain exchanges.

results.translated_text: Fui al bosque porque deseaba vivir de manera deliberada, enfrentarme únicamente a los aspectos esenciales de la vida y descubrir si podía aprender lo que tenía que enseñarme, y no, al llegar a la muerte, descubrir que no había vivido. No deseaba vivir una vida que no fuera vida, porque la vida es tan valiosa; ni tampoco deseaba practicar la resignación, a menos que fuera absolutamente necesario. Quería vivir intensamente y extraer toda la esencia de la vida.

scores.feedback: The translation preserves the philosophical meaning and structure well, with accurate rendering of Thoreau's key concepts like deliberate living and extracting life's essence. Minor deviations include 'valiosa' (valuable) for 'dear' and 'intensamente' (intensely) for 'deep,' which slightly soften the original's existential weight, and the style feels marginally more modern than Thoreau's 19th-century cadence.

results.translated_text: Llámame Ishmael. Hace algunos años, aunque no importa exactamente cuánto tiempo, sin mucho dinero en el bolsillo y sin nada en particular que me interesara en tierra firme, pensé que sería buena idea navegar un poco y explorar la parte acuática del mundo. Es una forma que tengo de combatir la melancolía y regular la circulación. Siempre que me siento decaído, cuando es un noviembre húmedo y sombrío en mi interior, considero que es el momento de ir al mar lo antes posible.

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning accurately but loses some archaic formality and poetic nuance of the original. Phrases like 'driving off the spleen' become 'combatir la melancolía' (more modern), and 'growing grim about the mouth' simplifies to 'decaído' (downcast), missing Melville's distinctive physical imagery and Victorian-era voice.

results.translated_text: È una verità universalmente riconosciuta che un uomo single, in possesso di una buona fortuna, debba necessariamente desiderare una moglie. Indipendentemente dalle convinzioni o dalle opinioni di un tale uomo al momento del suo arrivo in un determinato quartiere, questa verità è così radicata nelle menti delle famiglie circostanti che egli viene considerato come una sorta di proprietà legittima, destinata a una o l'altra delle loro figlie.

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning but loses Austen's ironic tone and elegant simplicity. The use of 'single' (English loanword) instead of 'celibe' and 'buona fortuna' instead of 'buona dote' are inaccurate choices; the phrasing is also more verbose and less graceful than the original's measured irony.

results.translated_text: Lo studio era pervaso dal ricco profumo delle rose, e quando la leggera brezza estiva si muoveva tra gli alberi del giardino, si poteva percepire attraverso la porta aperta il intenso aroma del lillà, oppure la più delicata fragranza del biancospino dai fiori rosa. Dalla sua posizione nell'angolo del divano ricoperto di pelle di ispirazione persiana, su cui era sdraiato, fumando, come al solito, innumerevoli sigarette, Lord Henry Wotton riusciva a intravedere il bagliore dei fiori di un laburnum, di un colore giallo miele.

scores.feedback: The translation preserves most meanings accurately, though 'pelle di ispirazione persiana' (leather of Persian inspiration) misses the original's 'saddle-bags' specificity, and 'honey-sweet and honey-coloured' is split awkwardly into separate clauses. The prose reads somewhat stiffly in Italian with minor awkward constructions that slightly diminish the aesthetic fluidity of Wilde's sensory prose.

results.translated_text: "Lei è stato in Afghanistan, immagino." "Come ha fatto a saperlo?", chiesi, stupito. "Non importa", rispose lui, ridendo tra sé. "La questione ora riguarda l'emoglobina. Non c'è dubbio che lei comprenda l'importanza di questa mia scoperta?" "È sicuramente interessante, dal punto di vista chimico", risposi, "ma dal punto di vista pratico..." "Ma signore, è la scoperta medico-legale più pratica degli ultimi anni. Non vede che ci fornisce un test infallibile per le macchie di sangue?"

scores.feedback: The translation accurately conveys the meaning and preserves the dialogue's intellectual tone, though 'più pratica' is slightly awkward compared to the original's emphasis on practicality. The formal Italian register is appropriate for the Victorian-era setting, though some phrasing could feel more naturally conversational.

results.translated_text: Sono andato nel bosco perché desideravo vivere intenzionalmente, affrontare solo gli aspetti essenziali della vita e scoprire se potessi imparare ciò che aveva da insegnare, e non, quando fossi giunto alla fine, rendermi conto di non aver vissuto. Non volevo vivere una vita che non fosse vita, perché la vita è così preziosa; né volevo esercitarmi nella rassegnazione, a meno che non fosse assolutamente necessario. Volevo vivere intensamente e assaporare ogni aspetto della vita.

scores.feedback: The translation captures most of Thoreau's meaning accurately, with natural Italian phrasing. However, the final phrase 'assaporare ogni aspetto della vita' (savor every aspect of life) loses the visceral power of 'suck out all the marrow of life,' diminishing the philosophical intensity and metaphorical richness characteristic of Thoreau's style.

results.translated_text: Chiamatemi Ishmael. Alcuni anni fa, non importa quanto tempo esattamente, trovandomi senza molto denaro e senza particolari interessi a terra, decisi di fare un breve viaggio per vedere le regioni marine del mondo. È un mio modo di scacciare la malinconia e regolare la circolazione. Ogni volta che mi sento cupo, ogni volta che la mia anima è avvolta da un novembre umido e piovoso, so che è giunto il momento di salpare il prima possibile.

scores.feedback: The translation captures the essential meaning and narrative voice reasonably well, but loses some of Melville's archaic formality and philosophical nuance. Phrases like 'driving off the spleen' become 'scacciare la malinconia' (a more modern interpretation), and the poetic rhythm of the original is somewhat flattened in the Italian rendering.

results.translated_text: É um fato universalmente reconhecido que um homem solteiro, possuidor de uma boa fortuna, deve estar em busca de uma esposa. Por mais desconhecidos que sejam os sentimentos ou opiniões de um homem assim ao se estabelecer em um novo bairro, essa verdade está tão profundamente enraizada na mente das famílias vizinhas, que ele é considerado a propriedade legítima de uma ou outra de suas filhas.

scores.feedback: The translation preserves the meaning and ironic tone of Austen's opening with excellent fidelity, though 'fato' is slightly less elegant than 'verdade' for the philosophical opening, and the flow is marginally less smooth than the original's rhythmic prose.

results.translated_text: O estúdio estava impregnado com o aroma intenso das rosas, e quando a brisa leve do verão soprava entre as árvores do jardim, o perfume forte do lilás ou a fragrância mais delicada do espinheiro rosado entravam pela porta aberta. Da esquina do divã de couro persa sobre o qual ele estava deitado, fumando, como de costume, inúmeros cigarros, Lord Henry Wotton podia vislumbrar o brilho das flores doces e amareladas de um amoreiral.

scores.feedback: The translation captures most of the meaning and sensory details effectively, though 'couro persa' (Persian leather) misrepresents 'saddle-bags' and 'amoreiral' is an awkward choice for laburnum. The aesthetic tone is mostly preserved but loses some of Wilde's lyrical precision in phrases like 'honey-sweet and honey-coloured' becoming simply 'doces e amareladas.'

results.translated_text: "Você esteve no Afeganistão, percebo." "Como você descobriu isso?", perguntei, surpreso. "Não importa", ele respondeu, rindo sozinho. "A questão agora é sobre a hemoglobina. Não há dúvida de que você percebe a importância dessa minha descoberta?" "É, sem dúvida, interessante do ponto de vista químico", respondi, "mas na prática..." "Ora, meu amigo, é a descoberta médico-legal mais prática dos últimos anos. Você não percebe que ela nos oferece um teste infalível para manchas de sangue?"

scores.feedback: The translation accurately conveys the meaning and maintains the dialogue's logical flow and character dynamics. Minor issues include 'rindo sozinho' (laughing alone) being slightly less idiomatic than 'rindo para si mesmo,' and the style shifts slightly from the original's Victorian formality, though 'Ora, meu amigo' effectively captures Holmes's characteristic tone.

results.translated_text: Fui para a floresta porque desejava viver de forma consciente, enfrentar apenas os fatos essenciais da vida e descobrir o que ela tinha a ensinar, para não, quando viesse a morrer, constatar que não havia vivido. Não desejava viver uma vida que não fosse vida, pois viver é algo precioso; nem desejava praticar a resignação, a menos que fosse absolutamente necessário. Queria viver intensamente e aproveitar ao máximo cada momento da vida.

scores.feedback: The translation captures most of the meaning accurately, with natural Portuguese phrasing, but loses some of Thoreau's distinctive metaphorical intensity—particularly 'suck out all the marrow of life' becomes the more generic 'aproveitar ao máximo cada momento,' diminishing the visceral power of the original philosophical voice.

results.translated_text: Chame-me Ishmael. Há alguns anos — não importa quantos, precisamente — tendo pouco ou nenhum dinheiro na carteira e nada em particular que me interessasse em terra, pensei que navegar um pouco seria uma boa ideia, para ver a parte aquática do mundo. É uma forma que tenho de afastar a melancolia e regular a circulação. Sempre que me sinto taciturno, sempre que é uma Novembro úmida e chuvosa na minha alma, considero que é hora de ir para o mar o mais rápido possível.

scores.feedback: The translation captures most of the meaning but loses some archaic formality and poetic resonance of the original. Phrases like 'seria uma boa ideia' feel too modern and casual compared to Melville's elevated tone, and 'Novembro úmida e chuvosa' awkwardly capitalizes the month while the construction feels stilted rather than lyrical.

results.translated_text: Jest powszechnie przyjęte, że każdy niezamężny mężczyzna, który posiada znaczny majątek, musi pragnąć żony. Niezależnie od tego, jak mało znane są uczucia i poglądy takiego mężczyzny, gdy po raz pierwszy wprowadza się do nowej okolicy, to prawda ta jest tak głęboko zakorzeniona w umysłach okolicznych rodzin, że uważa się go za przysługującą swojemu córce.

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning but loses some nuance in Austen's ironic opening and contains a grammatical error in the final clause ('przysługującą swojemu córce' is awkward). The formal tone is maintained but the distinctive Austenian voice and satirical bite are somewhat diminished.

results.translated_text: W pracowni unosił się intensywny zapach róż, a gdy letni wiatr delikatnie poruszał drzewami w ogrodzie, przez otwarte drzwi dobiegał ciężki aromat lilaków lub subtelniejszy zapach różowych cierni. Lord Henry Wotton, leżąc na orientalnym dywanie, z którego wychodziły rzemienie, i paląc, jak zwykle, niezliczone papierosy, z rogu tej kanapy mógł dostrzec blask miodowo-słodkich i miodowo-żółtych kwiatów akacji.

scores.feedback: The translation captures most meaning accurately but loses some aesthetic precision: 'divan of Persian saddle-bags' becomes 'orientalny dywan, z którego wychodziły rzemienie' (oriental carpet with straps), and 'laburnum' is rendered as 'akacja' (acacia), which are botanically different plants. The sensory richness is mostly preserved, though the poetic flow is slightly diminished by awkward phrasing in places.

results.translated_text: "Być może był pan w Afganistanie", zauważyłem. "Jak pan to wiedział?", zapytałem z niedowierzaniem. "To nie ma znaczenia", odparł, cicho się śmiejąc. "Teraz chodzi o hemoglobinę. Czy dostrzegasz znaczenie tego mojego odkrycia?" "Oczywiście, jest to interesujące z punktu widzenia chemii", odpowiedziałem, "ale w praktyce..." "Panie, to jest jedno z najbardziej praktycznych odkryć medyczno-prawnych od lat. Czy nie widzisz, że daje nam ono niezawodny sposób identyfikacji śladów krwi?"

scores.feedback: The translation captures the basic meaning but contains significant errors: the opening attribution is reversed (Holmes's observation becomes the narrator's), inconsistent pronoun usage (mixing formal 'pan' with informal 'ty'), and awkward phrasing that disrupts the natural dialogue flow. The Victorian tone and Holmesian character voice are diminished.

results.translated_text: Poszedłem do lasu, ponieważ chciałem żyć w sposób celowy, skoncentrować się tylko na najważniejszych aspektach życia i sprawdzić, czy mogę nauczyć się, czego on ma do zaoferowania, i nie odkryć, gdy nadejdzie moja śmierć, że nie żyłem. Nie chciałem żyć w sposób, który nie byłby prawdziwym życiem, ponieważ życie jest tak cenne; ani nie chciałem praktykować rezygnacji, chyba że byłaby to absolutnie konieczne. Chciałem żyć głęboko i w pełni wykorzystać każdy moment.

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core philosophical meaning well, with natural Polish phrasing, but loses some of Thoreau's poetic intensity, particularly in the final phrase where 'suck out all the marrow of life' becomes the more mundane 'w pełni wykorzystać każdy moment,' diminishing the visceral, metaphorical power of the original.

results.translated_text: Nazywajcie mnie Izmael. Kilka lat temu – nie ważne, dokładnie ile – będąc osobą, która nie dysponowała zbyt dużymi zasobami finansowymi i nie mającą żadnych szczególnych zainteresowań na lądzie, postanowiłem popłynąć i zobaczyć wodne zakątki świata. To mój sposób na pozbycie się melancholii i poprawę krążenia. Kiedy tylko zaczynam czuć się przygnębiony, kiedy w mojej duszy następuje wilgotny, deszczowy listopad, wiem, że najwyższy czas jak najszybciej wyjść na morze.

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning but loses the archaic, intimate voice of the original through overly formal phrasing ('będąc osobą, która nie dysponowała zbyt dużymi zasobami finansowymi' for 'having little or no money') and misses the poetic rhythm and colloquial warmth characteristic of Melville's opening. The style shifts from conversational to bureaucratic, diminishing the literary impact.

results.translated_text: 人们常说，一个拥有良好财富的单身男子，必然渴望拥有妻子。 即使我们不太了解这样一位男子刚来到一个新社区时的感受和想法，但这个真理已经深深地根植于周围家庭的观念中，他们认为他理应属于他们女儿中的某一个。

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning accurately but loses some of Austen's ironic bite and formal elegance. The phrase '人们常说' (people often say) weakens the authorial voice of the original's declarative opening, and the rendering misses the subtle irony of 'rightful property' which should carry more satirical weight.

results.translated_text: 工作室里弥漫着浓郁的玫瑰香味，当夏日微风吹过花园中的树木时，从敞开的门里会飘进来丁香的强烈气味，或者蔷薇花更为清新的芬芳。亨利·沃顿勋爵躺在波斯风格的沙发上，一边抽着他常抽的无数支香烟，从沙发角落里，他隐约能看到金黄色的槐树花朵，那香味甜如蜜。

scores.feedback: The translation captures most key meanings and sensory details accurately, though 'saddle-bags' is rendered as 'style' rather than the specific furniture detail, and some nuance of Wilde's layered aesthetic descriptions is flattened. The prose reads naturally in Chinese but lacks the rhythmic elegance and precise sensory gradation of the original's aestheticist style.

results.translated_text: “你曾经去过阿富汗，我猜得没错。”我惊讶地问道：“你究竟是怎么知道的？” “别在意，”他自嘲地笑了笑。“现在的问题是关于血红蛋白。你肯定能理解我这个发现的重要性，对吧？” “从化学角度来说，这无疑很有趣，”我回答道，“但从实际应用上来说……” “我的天啊，这可是近年来最重要的医学法律发现。你没看到，这能为我们提供一个判断血迹的绝对可靠的方法吗？”

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning and dialogue structure well, but has some issues: the opening attribution is reversed (should be Holmes speaking first, not the narrator asking), 'self-chuckling' is awkwardly rendered as 'self-mocking laughter,' and some Victorian formality is lost. The scientific terminology is accurate and the logical flow is preserved, though the natural conversational rhythm could be smoother.

results.translated_text: 我来到森林，是因为我渴望过一种有意识的生活，只关注人生最重要的事实，看看我是否能够从中学习，并且不希望在生命的尽头，发现自己从未真正地活过。我不想过一种虚伪的生活，因为生命是如此珍贵；我也不想轻易地接受命运，除非那是绝对必要的。我想要深入地体验生活，并从中汲取一切精华。

scores.feedback: The translation captures the philosophical essence and meaning of Thoreau's passage well, with faithful rendering of key concepts like deliberate living and extracting life's marrow. Minor accuracy issues include 'what was not life' becoming '虚伪的生活' (false/hypocritical life) which slightly shifts the original meaning, and the fluency is generally natural though some phrasings feel slightly more formal than Thoreau's conversational tone.

results.translated_text: 请叫我以实玛利。 几年前——具体时间不重要——我身无分文，而且在陆地上没有任何特别吸引我的事物，于是我决定出海，看看这片广阔的水域。 这是一种我排解烦闷、调节身心的办法。 每当我感到心情低落，或者我的内心像十一月一样阴冷潮湿，我就觉得是时候尽快出海了。

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning and narrative voice effectively, though some nuances are lost—'driving off the spleen' becomes generic '排解烦闷' rather than preserving the specific medical/philosophical reference, and the archaic, contemplative tone is somewhat modernized. The prose reads naturally in Chinese but lacks the distinctive Melvillean voice and poetic weight of the original.

results.translated_text: 自古以來，人們普遍認為，一位擁有豐厚財產的單身男子，必然渴望找到一位妻子。 即使我們對這樣一位男子在初次進入一個社區時的感受和想法知之甚少，但這個觀念已經深深地根植於周圍家庭的思維中，他們認為他理應屬於他們女兒中的某一位。

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning accurately but loses some of Austen's distinctive ironic voice and the elegant simplicity of the original opening. The phrase '自古以來' (since ancient times) adds unnecessary historical weight, and the translation becomes slightly more verbose than the original, diluting the sharp irony of the famous opening line.

results.translated_text: 畫室裡瀰漫著濃郁的玫瑰香味，當夏日微風吹拂過花園中的樹木時，從敞開的門口傳來丁香花的濃烈氣息，或是薔薇花更為淡雅的香氣。 亨利·沃頓勳爵躺在波斯皮革製成的沙發上，按照他的一貫習慣，點燃一支又一支的香菸。從這個位置，他可以勉強看到一棵金黃色槐樹上，散發著甜蜜氣味的、金黃色的花朵。

scores.feedback: The translation captures the sensory richness and general meaning well, but loses some aesthetic precision: 'pink-flowering thorn' becomes '薔薇花' (rose), and 'laburnum' is rendered as '槐樹' (Chinese scholar tree) rather than the correct golden chain tree, altering the visual imagery. The prose reads naturally in Chinese but lacks the flowing, lyrical quality of Wilde's original aesthetic style.

results.translated_text:
“您曾經去過阿富汗，我猜得對。” 我驚訝地問道：“您怎麼知道的？”
“別在意，” 他自嘲地笑了笑。“現在的問題是關於血紅蛋白。您肯定明白我這個發現的重要性，對吧？”
“這的確在化學上很有趣，” 我回答道，“但從實際應用來看——”
“我的天啊，這可是近年來最實用的醫學和法律發現。您沒看到這能為我們提供一個絕對可靠的血液檢測方法嗎？”

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning and scientific discussion accurately, though some nuances are lost (e.g., 'Never mind' becomes 'Don't mind' which slightly shifts tone, and 'self-chuckling' becomes 'self-mocking'). The dialogue flows reasonably well in Chinese but reads somewhat more formal than the original's conversational ease, and Holmes's characteristic deductive confidence is slightly diminished.

results.translated_text: 我前往森林，因為我渴望過一種有意識的生活，只關注人生中最重要的事情，並想看看它能教我什麼，以免在臨終之際，發現我並未真正地生活。 我不願虛度光陰，因為生命如此珍貴；我也不想勉強接受任何事情，除非那絕對是必要的。 我想要深入地生活，並盡情地體驗生命的精髓。

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core philosophical meaning and existential urgency of Thoreau's passage well, with natural Chinese phrasing. Minor accuracy issues include 'deliberately' rendered as 'conscious' rather than 'intentional,' and 'suck out all the marrow' softened to 'fully experience,' which slightly diminishes the visceral intensity of the original metaphor.

results.translated_text: 請稱呼我為以實瑪利。幾年前——具體時間不詳——我身無分文，而且陸地上也沒有任何特別吸引我的事物，於是我想去航海，看看這個世界的海洋部分。這是我排解煩惱、調節身心的習慣。每當我感到心情低落，或者我的內心像十一月那樣陰濕潮濕，我就認為是時候盡快出海了。

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning and narrative voice reasonably well, though some nuances are lost—'driving off the spleen' becomes generic '排解煩惱' rather than preserving the medical/humoral philosophy undertone, and the poetic metaphor 'damp, drizzly November in my soul' is somewhat flattened. The archaic literary tone is partially maintained but lacks the distinctive cadence and philosophical weight of Melville's original prose.

results.translated_text: 世間一般に、良縁を得ている未婚男性は、必ず結婚を望んでいる、という考えは広く浸透しているものです。そのような男性が初めてある地域に移り住む際、彼の個人的な感情や考えがどれほど知られていようとも、その事実は周囲の家庭の心に深く根付いており、彼は何らかの娘の正当な「所有物」と見なされるのです。

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning and ironic premise effectively, though 'good fortune' becomes 'good match' (良縁) which slightly shifts the emphasis from wealth to marriageability. The ironic tone is present but somewhat diluted by the more straightforward Japanese phrasing, and the famous opening's distinctive rhythm is lost in translation.

results.translated_text: スタジオはバラの濃厚な香りで満たされており、夏の穏やかな風が庭の木々を吹き抜けると、開いたドアからライラックの重い香りや、より繊細なピンク色のトゲツノキの香りが漂ってくる。ペルシャの鞍を模したソファの隅に横たわり、いつものように無数のタバコを吸っていたロード・ヘンリー・ウォットンは、そこから黄金色で甘い花の蜜のような香りのするナナカマドの木の花の輝きをわずかに見ることができた。

scores.feedback: The translation captures most key elements and sensory details, but contains some inaccuracies (laburnum misidentified as ナナカマド/rowan, awkward phrasing with 'saddle-bags' as 鞍を模した). The prose reads somewhat stiffly in Japanese, losing some of Wilde's flowing aesthetic elegance and the delicate musicality of the original sensory descriptions.

results.translated_text: 「あなたはアフガニスタンにいらっしゃったのですね。」と彼は言いました。「どうしてそれを知っているんですか？」私は驚いて尋ねました。「気にしないでください」と彼は独り言のように笑いながら言いました。「今重要なのはヘモグロビンについてです。私のこの発見の重要性をご理解いただけていることでしょう？」私は答えました。「化学的には興味深いことでしょうが、実用的な面では…」。「いいえ、これは長年で最も実用的な医学・法医学的な発見なのです。それが血液の痕跡に対する絶対的な検査方法になるということに気づかないのですか？」

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning and dialogue structure accurately, but the Japanese reads somewhat stiffly with overly formal constructions (「いらっしゃったのですね」, 「ご理解いただけていることでしょう」) that don't match the original's conversational tone. The character voices lack distinction and naturalness compared to Doyle's original dialogue.

results.translated_text: 私は、意識的に生き、人生の本質的な事柄にのみ向き合い、それが教えてくれることを学び、そして死を迎える時、自分が生きていないと後悔しないように、という願いを込めて、森へ行きました。私は、生きていることと違うことを生きることは望まず、生きることはかけがえのないものです。また、必要に迫られた場合でなければ、諦めを実践することも望みませんでした。私は、深く生き、人生のすべてを味わい尽くしたいと考えたのです。

scores.feedback: The translation captures most of Thoreau's philosophical meaning but loses some rhetorical force and poetic rhythm. The sentence structure becomes more fragmented and explanatory rather than maintaining the original's flowing, deliberative tone; phrases like 'suck out all the marrow' become diluted into 'taste thoroughly,' and the parallel construction of the original is somewhat compromised.

results.translated_text: 私の名前はイシュメールと呼んでください。数年前のことですが、正確な年数は覚えていません。私はほとんどお金を持っておらず、陸上で特に興味を引くものもなかったので、少し船旅をして、世界の海を訪ねることにしました。これは、私の気分転換をするための、そして血行を促進するための方法なのです。私が口元が暗くなりそうになったとき、つまり、私の心にどんよりとした、霧雨のような十一月が訪れたと感じたとき、私はできるだけ早く海に出るべきだと考えるのです。

scores.feedback: The translation captures most key meanings but loses some nuance (e.g., 'spleen' as melancholy is rendered literally as circulation regulation). The Japanese reads somewhat stiffly and awkwardly, particularly in the final sentence, failing to match the archaic literary elegance and philosophical tone of Melville's original prose.

results.translated_text: 모든 진실 중에서도 가장 널리 알려진 진실은, 재산을 많이 가진 미혼 남자는 반드시 아내를 원한다는 것이다. 그런 남자가 처음으로 어떤 동네에 들어설 때, 그의 생각이나 감정이 얼마나 알려져 있는지와 상관없이, 그 진실은 주변 가족들의 마음속에 깊이 뿌리내려 있어서, 그는 그 가족 딸들 중 누구에게나 정당하게 속해야 할 존재로 여겨진다.

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning but loses Austen's iconic ironic opening and the subtle social commentary. The phrasing is somewhat awkward ("모든 진실 중에서도 가장 널리 알려진 진실은" is redundant) and doesn't match the elegant, tongue-in-cheek tone of the original.

results.translated_text: 스튜디오 안은 장미의 풍부한 향기로 가득 차 있었고, 여름의 부드러운 바람이 정원의 나무들 사이를 스치고 지나갈 때마다, 열린 문을 통해 라일락의 짙은 향기나, 더 섬세한 분홍빛 가시나무의 향기가 실려 들어왔다. 그는 페르시아 마상의 가죽으로 덮인 다리베드에 누워, 늘 하던 대로 수많은 담배를 피우고 있었는데, 그 다리베드의 한쪽 모서리에 있는 자리에서 그는 꿀처럼 달콤하고 꿀빛을 띤 금낭화의 꽃잎이 반짝이는 모습을 희미하게 볼 수 있었다.

scores.feedback: The translation captures most of the sensory details and meaning accurately, but struggles with fluency and style preservation. The sentence structure becomes awkward and overly complex in Korean, particularly in the second sentence, and some aesthetic nuances of Wilde's prose—such as the lyrical quality and rhythmic flow—are lost in the translation.

results.translated_text: "당신은 아프가니스탄에 가셨던 것 같습니다." 저는 놀라움에 질문했습니다. "어떻게 그걸 아셨죠?" 그는 킥킥 웃으며 대답했습니다. "그건 중요하지 않습니다." "물론 화학적으로 흥미로운 발견일 겁니다." 제가 말했습니다. "하지만 실용적인 측면에서—" "아니, 정말 놀라운 일입니다. 수 년 만에 가장 실용적인 의학 및 법의학 분야의 발견이죠. 이 발견이 혈흔 검사에 있어서 완벽하게 신뢰할 수 있는 방법을 제공한다는 것을 이해하지 못하시겠습니까?"

scores.feedback: The translation captures the basic meaning but has significant structural issues: dialogue attribution is confused (Watson's question appears before Holmes's response), and the flow is disrupted. The tone shifts awkwardly, losing Holmes's characteristic arrogance and Watson's measured astonishment, while some phrasing feels overly formal or awkward for natural Korean dialogue.

results.translated_text: 저는 삶의 본질적인 측면만을 마주하고, 그것이 가르쳐주는 것을 배우며, 죽음에 이르렀을 때 제가 진정으로 살아왔다는 것을 깨닫고 싶었기에 숲으로 갔습니다. 저는 삶이 아니라고 느끼는 것을 살아갈 생각은 없었고, 삶은 너무나 소중합니다. 또한, 꼭 필요하지 않은 한 체념하는 삶을 살고 싶지도 않았습니다. 저는 깊이 있게 살고, 삶의 모든 풍요로움을 경험하고 싶었습니다.

scores.feedback: The translation captures most of the meaning but loses some key nuances: 'deliberately' is omitted from the opening, and 'suck out all the marrow' becomes the softer 'experience all abundance,' diminishing Thoreau's visceral intensity. The fluency is generally natural Korean, though the sentence structure becomes more fragmented than the original's flowing rhetoric, and the philosophical weight of phrases like 'living is so dear' feels slightly diluted.

results.translated_text: 제 이름은 이즈마일입니다. 몇 년 전—정확히 얼마나 된 건지는 중요하지 않습니다—저는 돈이 거의 없고, 육지에서 특별히 흥미로운 일도 없다고 생각했습니다. 그래서 잠시 항해를 하며 세상의 바다를 둘러보려 했습니다. 제가 늘 취하는 방법이죠. 우울감을 해소하고 혈액 순환을 돕는 방법입니다. 제가 입술 주변이 굳어지거나, 마음속에 음울하고 흐린 11월이 찾아올 때, 저는 가능한 한 빨리 바다로 나가야 한다고 생각합니다.

scores.feedback: The translation captures the basic meaning but loses the archaic, introspective tone of Melville's prose. Phrases like '제 이름은 이즈마일입니다' (formal/modern) and '중요하지 않습니다' lack the intimate, colloquial quality of 'Call me Ishmael' and 'never mind.' The poetic metaphors ('damp, drizzly November in my soul') become more literal and less evocative, and the overall rhythm and cadence of the original's distinctive voice are diminished.

results.translated_text: Ai cũng biết một sự thật hiển nhiên: một người đàn ông độc thân, sở hữu một gia tài lớn, tất yếu sẽ cần một người vợ. Dù cảm xúc và quan điểm của người đàn ông đó có thể là gì khi mới chuyển đến một khu phố, sự thật này đã ăn sâu vào tâm trí của các gia đình xung quanh, đến nỗi anh ta được coi là "tài sản" hợp pháp của một trong những cô con gái của họ.

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning and ironic tone effectively, with good preservation of the logical structure. However, 'Ai cũng biết' (everyone knows) slightly softens the universal authority of the original opening, and some phrasing feels slightly more literal than natural Vietnamese prose would be.

results.translated_text: Xưởng vẽ tràn ngập mùi thơm nồng của hoa hồng, và khi làn gió mùa hè nhẹ nhàng thổi qua những hàng cây trong vườn, hương thơm đậm đà của hoa tử đinh hương hoặc hương thơm tinh tế hơn của hoa dâm bụt nở hoa hồng lan vào từ cửa mở. Từ góc chiếc ghế dài bọc da thuộc Ba Tư mà Lord Henry Wotton đang nằm trên đó, hút vô số điếu thuốc như thường lệ, ông có thể thoáng thấy ánh sáng của những bông hoa laburnum với màu vàng mật ong.

scores.feedback: The translation captures most of the meaning and sensory details, but has some issues: 'hoa dâm bụt nở hoa hồng' is awkward for 'pink-flowering thorn,' and 'chiếc ghế dài bọc da' (upholstered chair) doesn't convey 'divan of Persian saddle-bags' accurately. The aesthetic, luxurious tone is somewhat diluted by less elegant phrasing, and the poetic flow of Wilde's prose is not fully preserved in the Vietnamese rendering.

results.translated_text: "Tôi đoán là bạn đã từng ở Afghanistan," ông ấy nói. "Làm sao ông biết được?" Tôi hỏi, kinh ngạc. "Không cần biết," ông đáp, cười khúc khích. "Bây giờ, chúng ta hãy nói về hemoglobin. Chắc hẳn bạn nhận thấy tầm quan trọng của phát hiện này của tôi?" "Nó chắc chắn rất thú vị về mặt hóa học," tôi trả lời, "nhưng về mặt ứng dụng—" "Nghe này, đó là một phát hiện y học pháp y thực tế nhất trong nhiều năm. Bạn không thấy rằng nó cho chúng ta một phương pháp kiểm tra máu không thể sai sót?"

scores.feedback: The translation accurately conveys the meaning and captures Holmes's deductive confidence and Watson's astonishment, though some phrasing feels slightly stiff (e.g., 'phương pháp kiểm tra máu không thể sai sót' is more literal than natural). The dialogue structure and character dynamics are well-preserved, but minor word choices could be more idiomatic for smoother Vietnamese prose.

results.translated_text: Tôi đến sống trong rừng vì tôi mong muốn sống một cuộc đời có ý nghĩa, đối diện với những sự thật thiết yếu của cuộc sống, và xem liệu tôi có thể học được những gì mà cuộc sống có thể dạy cho tôi hay không, và không phải đến khi chết mới nhận ra rằng mình đã không thực sự sống. Tôi không muốn sống một cuộc đời hời hợt, bởi vì cuộc sống thật quý giá; cũng như tôi không muốn chấp nhận sự cam chịu, trừ khi đó là điều hoàn toàn cần thiết. Tôi muốn sống một cuộc đời sâu sắc, trọn vẹn và tận hưởng tất cả những giá trị mà cuộc sống mang lại.

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning and philosophical intent well, though some nuances are lost—particularly the vivid metaphor 'suck out all the marrow of life' becomes the more abstract 'tận hưởng tất cả những giá trị,' and 'living is so dear' shifts slightly in emphasis. The Vietnamese reads naturally but occasionally feels more explanatory than the original's poetic compression, and the distinctive voice of Thoreau's deliberate, aphoristic style is somewhat softened.

results.translated_text: Hãy gọi tôi là Ishmael. Vài năm trước đây – không quan trọng chính xác là bao nhiêu năm – khi tôi không có nhiều tiền và không có điều gì đặc biệt thu hút tôi trên đất liền, tôi nghĩ rằng mình nên đi biển một chút để khám phá thế giới dưới nước. Đó là một cách mà tôi thường làm để giải tỏa những cảm xúc tiêu cực và điều hòa lưu thông. Bất cứ khi nào tôi cảm thấy buồn bã, khi nào tâm hồn tôi trở nên ẩm ướt và u ám như những ngày tháng Mười Một mưa drizzle, tôi biết rằng đã đến lúc phải ra khơi càng sớm càng tốt.

scores.feedback: The translation captures most of the meaning but loses some nuance—'driving off the spleen' becomes generic 'negative emotions,' and the poetic metaphor of 'damp, drizzly November in my soul' is awkwardly literalized. The archaic, introspective tone of the original is diminished, and phrases like 'hãy gọi' and 'drizzle' (left untranslated) disrupt the natural flow of Vietnamese prose.

results.translated_text: เป็นความจริงที่ได้รับการยอมรับกันโดยทั่วไปว่า ชายโสดที่มีฐานะร่ำรวย จะต้องกำลังมองหาภรรยา ไม่ว่าชายผู้นั้นจะมีจิตใจหรือความคิดเห็นอย่างไรเมื่อเข้ามาอยู่ในย่านใหม่ ความจริงนี้ฝังรากลึกอยู่ในจิตใจของครอบครัวในละแวกนั้น จนทำให้ชายผู้นั้นถูกมองว่าเป็นสมบัติโดยชอบธรรมของลูกสาวคนใดคนหนึ่งในครอบครัวเหล่านั้น

scores.feedback: The translation accurately captures the meaning and ironic tone of Austen's opening, with good preservation of the logical structure and satirical commentary on marriage customs. Minor fluency issues arise from slightly awkward phrasing ("จะต้องกำลังมองหา" could be more concise) and the style feels somewhat formal-academic rather than maintaining Austen's distinctive narrative voice and wit.

results.translated_text: ห้องทำงานอบอวลไปด้วยกลิ่นหอมของดอกกุหลาบ และเมื่อสายลมฤดูร้อนพัดผ่านต้นไม้ในสวน ประตูที่เปิดอยู่ก็ส่งกลิ่นหอมฉุนของดอกไลแลค หรือกลิ่นหอมละมุนของดอกพุ่มไม้สีชมพูเข้ามาในห้อง จากมุมของโซฟาบุหนังลายเปอร์เซียที่ลอร์ดเฮนรี่ วอตตันนอนอยู่ เขาสามารถมองเห็นแสงระยิบระยับของดอกลาบูร์นัม ซึ่งมีสีเหลืองทองและมีกลิ่นหอมหวานเหมือนน้ำผึ้ง

scores.feedback: The translation captures the sensory richness and aesthetic focus of the original, preserving most key details about scents and visual imagery. Minor issues include slight awkwardness in phrasing (e.g., 'โซฟาบุหนังลายเปอร์เซีย' for 'divan of Persian saddle-bags' loses the specific cultural reference), and the flow could be more elegant in places, though the overall aesthetic sensibility is maintained.

results.translated_text: "ผมสังเกตว่าคุณเคยอยู่ในอัฟกานิสถานมาแล้วสินะ" ผมถามด้วยความประหลาดใจ "คุณรู้ได้อย่างไร?" เขาหัวเราะเบาๆ แล้วตอบว่า "ไม่สำคัญหรอก" "ตอนนี้คำถามคือเรื่องฮีโมโกลบิน คุณคงเห็นความสำคัญของการค้นพบนี้ของผมใช่ไหม?" ผมตอบว่า "แน่นอนว่ามันน่าสนใจในเชิงเคมี แต่ในแง่ของการนำไปใช้จริง..." "คุณรู้ไหมว่ามันเป็นการค้นพบทางการแพทย์และกฎหมายที่ใช้งานได้จริงมากที่สุดในช่วงหลายปีที่ผ่านมา? คุณไม่เห็นหรือว่ามันช่วยให้เรามีวิธีการทดสอบที่แม่นยำสำหรับคราบเลือดได้?"

scores.feedback: The translation captures most of the meaning accurately but has some structural issues: the dialogue attribution is confused ("ผมถามด้วยความประหลาดใจ" should follow the question, not precede it), and the conversational flow is disrupted. While the technical terms are handled well, the natural rhythm and Victorian-era formality of the original dialogue are somewhat lost in the Thai rendering.

results.translated_text: ฉันเดินทางไปยังป่า เพราะฉันปรารถนาที่จะใช้ชีวิตอย่างมีสติ เพื่อเผชิญหน้ากับความจริงที่จำเป็นของชีวิต และเพื่อเรียนรู้สิ่งที่มันสามารถสอนได้ และไม่ใช่เพื่อที่จะเมื่อถึงแก่ความตายแล้ว ฉันจะพบว่าฉันไม่ได้ใช้ชีวิตอย่างแท้จริง ฉันไม่ได้ต้องการที่จะใช้ชีวิตในสิ่งที่ไม่ใช่ชีวิต เพราะชีวิตนั้นมีค่ามาก และฉันก็ไม่ได้ต้องการที่จะฝึกฝนการยอมจำนน เว้นแต่ว่ามันจะเป็นสิ่งที่จำเป็นอย่างยิ่ง ฉันต้องการที่จะใช้ชีวิตอย่างลึกซึ้ง และดื่มด่ำกับแก่นแท้ของชีวิตให้เต็มที่

scores.feedback: The translation captures Thoreau's philosophical meaning and key concepts (deliberate living, essential facts, deep living) with good fidelity, though some nuances like the paradoxical rhythm of 'living is so dear' feel slightly flattened. The Thai prose is generally natural but occasionally awkward in structure, particularly in the long conditional clause about death, which disrupts the contemplative flow of the original.

results.translated_text: เรียกฉันว่าอิชมาเอล บางปีที่ผ่านมา—ไม่ว่าจะเป็นเวลานานแค่ไหนก็ตาม—ด้วยเงินในกระเป๋าที่เหลือน้อยมาก หรือไม่มีอะไรเป็นพิเศษที่น่าสนใจบนบก ฉันจึงคิดที่จะออกเรือไปสำรวจส่วนต่างๆ ของโลกบ้าง นี่เป็นวิธีหนึ่งที่ฉันใช้ในการคลายความหงุดหงิดและปรับสมดุลการไหลเวียนโลหิต เมื่อไหร่ก็ตามที่ฉันรู้สึกเศร้าหรือหดหู่ เมื่อไหร่ก็ตามที่จิตใจของฉันเหมือนกับเดือนพฤศจิกายนที่ชื้นและมีฝนพรำ ฉันถือว่าถึงเวลาแล้วที่จะต้องออกทะเลโดยเร็วที่สุด

scores.feedback: The translation captures most of the meaning accurately, including the famous opening and philosophical reflections on melancholy, but loses some of the archaic literary elegance and poetic metaphors of the original. The Thai phrasing feels somewhat literal and modern rather than maintaining Melville's distinctive 19th-century narrative voice and introspective tone.

results.translated_text: यह एक सर्वमान्य सत्य है कि एक अविवाहित व्यक्ति जिसके पास अच्छी संपत्ति हो, उसे निश्चित रूप से एक पत्नी की आवश्यकता होती है। चाहे ऐसे व्यक्ति की भावनाओं या विचारों के बारे में कुछ भी ज्ञात हो, जब वह पहली बार किसी इलाके में प्रवेश करता है, फिर भी यह सत्य आसपास के परिवारों के मन में इतना गहराई से स्थापित है कि उसे उनकी बेटियों में से किसी एक का "अधिकार" माना जाता है।

scores.feedback: The translation accurately conveys the core meaning and ironic premise, though 'अधिकार' (right/claim) slightly weakens the satirical edge of 'rightful property.' The Hindi reads naturally but lacks some of the original's elegant ironic tone and the subtle social commentary embedded in Austen's phrasing.

results.translated_text: कमरा गुलाबों की गहरी खुशबू से भरा हुआ था, और जब हल्की ग्रीष्मकालीन हवा बगीचे के पेड़ों के बीच से बहती थी, तो खुले दरवाजे से लैवेंडर की तीखी खुशबू या गुलाबी फूलों वाले कांटेदार पौधे की हल्की सुगंध आती थी। फ़ारसी चर्म के बने सोफे के कोने से, जहाँ लॉर्ड हेनरी वॉटन लेटे हुए थे, सिगरेट पी रहे थे, जैसा कि उनकी आदत थी, वे लैंबर्नम के शहद जैसी मीठी और सुनहरे रंग के फूलों की चमक को देख सकते थे।

scores.feedback: The translation captures most of the sensory content but contains a significant error: 'lilac' is mistranslated as 'लैवेंडर' (lavender) rather than 'बकायन' or 'नीलकंठ' (lilac), altering the botanical specificity. The prose is somewhat awkward and loses the flowing, aesthetic elegance of Wilde's original through clunky phrasing and structural issues.

results.translated_text: "मुझे लगता है कि आप अफ़गानिस्तान में रहे हैं।" मैंने आश्चर्य से पूछा, "आपको यह कैसे पता चला?" "कोई बात नहीं," उसने खुद से मुस्कुराते हुए कहा। "अब सवाल है हीमोग्लोबिन का। मुझे यकीन है कि आप मेरे इस खोज के महत्व को समझते होंगे?" "यह निश्चित रूप से रासायनिक रूप से दिलचस्प है," मैंने उत्तर दिया, "लेकिन व्यावहारिक रूप से—" "अरे, यह पिछले कई सालों की सबसे व्यावहारिक चिकित्सा-कानूनी खोज है। क्या आप नहीं देख रहे हैं कि यह हमें रक्त के धब्बों के लिए एक अचूक परीक्षण प्रदान करता है?"

scores.feedback: The translation accurately captures the meaning and maintains the logical flow of the dialogue, with proper preservation of Holmes's deductive confidence and Watson's astonishment. Minor fluency issues include slightly awkward phrasing in places (e.g., 'खुद से मुस्कुराते हुए' could be more natural) and the style feels somewhat formal, losing some of the conversational ease of the original English.

results.translated_text: मैं जंगल में गया क्योंकि मैं जानबूझकर जीना चाहता था, जीवन के केवल बुनियादी सत्यों का सामना करना चाहता था, और यह देखना चाहता था कि क्या मैं उनसे कुछ सीख सकता हूँ, और यह नहीं कि जब मैं मरने आऊँ तो मुझे यह पता चले कि मैंने वास्तव में जीवन नहीं जिया। मैं वह जीवन नहीं जीना चाहता था जो जीवन नहीं था; जीवन बहुत अनमोल है; और न ही मैं त्याग का अभ्यास करना चाहता था, सिवाय इसके कि जब यह बिल्कुल आवश्यक हो। मैं गहराई से जीना चाहता था और जीवन के हर सार को निचोड़ना चाहता था।

scores.feedback: The translation captures the philosophical meaning and core ideas of Thoreau's passage accurately, with faithful rendering of key concepts like deliberate living and essential facts. However, the fluency is slightly compromised by some awkward constructions (e.g., 'जब मैं मरने आऊँ' is somewhat stiff), and the poetic intensity of phrases like 'suck out all the marrow of life' loses some of its visceral power in 'जीवन के हर सार को निचोड़ना,' which reads more literally than evocatively.

results.translated_text: मुझे इशमाएल कहिए। कुछ साल पहले—ठीक कितने साल, यह कोई बात नहीं—मेरे पास बहुत कम पैसे थे और मुझे किनारे पर कोई ऐसी चीज़ भी नहीं मिल रही थी जिससे मेरा मन लगे, इसलिए मैंने सोचा कि मैं थोड़ा यात्रा करूँ और दुनिया के समुद्री हिस्से को देखूँ। यह मेरा तनाव दूर करने और शरीर में रक्त संचार को सामान्य रखने का एक तरीका है। जब भी मुझे लगता है कि मैं उदास हो रहा हूँ; जब भी मेरे भीतर एक नमी भरा, उदास नवंबर जैसा माहौल होता है, तो मैं समझता हूँ कि अब समुद्र में जाने का समय आ गया है।

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning accurately but loses some of Melville's philosophical nuance and archaic tone. Phrases like 'driving off the spleen' become more literal ('तनाव दूर करना'), and the poetic rhythm of the original's introspective voice is diminished by more straightforward Hindi phrasing.

results.translated_text: এটা সর্বজনবিদিত সত্য যে, একজন সম্পদশালী অবিবাহিত পুরুষকে অবশ্যই একজন স্ত্রীর প্রয়োজন। একজন ব্যক্তি কোনো নতুন এলাকায় প্রবেশ করার সময় তার অনুভূতি বা চিন্তাভাবনা সম্পর্কে যতই কম জানা যাক না কেন, এই সত্যটি আশেপাশের পরিবারগুলোর মনে এতটাই গেঁথে আছে যে, তাকে তাদের daughters-দের মধ্যে একজনের "অধিকার" হিসেবে গণ্য করা হয়।

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning but loses Austen's ironic tone and formal elegance. The phrase 'daughters-দের' is awkwardly left partially in English, and 'অধিকার' (property/right) doesn't fully convey the satirical edge of 'rightful property.' The sentence structure is more literal than literary.

results.translated_text: স্টুডিওটি গোলাপের তীব্র সুবাসে পরিপূর্ণ ছিল, এবং যখন হালকা গ্রীষ্মের বাতাস বাগানের গাছপালার মধ্যে বয়ে যেত, তখন খোলা দরজা দিয়ে লিলি ফুলের ভারী ঘ্রাণ অথবা গোলাপ জাতীয় গুল্মের হালকা সুবাস ভেসে আসত। পার্সিয়ান চামড়ার তৈরি divan-এর কোণে, যেখানে তিনি শুয়ে ছিলেন এবং স্বাভাবিকভাবেই অসংখ্য সিগারেট খাচ্ছিলেন, লর্ড হেনরি Вотটন লতাবনের মধু-গন্ধযুক্ত, সোনালী রঙের ফুলগুলোর সামান্য ঝলক দেখতে পেতেন।

scores.feedback: The translation captures the basic meaning but loses aesthetic precision: 'lilac' becomes 'lily' (ফুল), 'pink-flowering thorn' is vaguely rendered as 'rose-like shrub', and the sensory layering is flattened. Fluency suffers from awkward constructions ('Persian leather-made divan'), untranslated English words (divan, Вотton in Cyrillic), and loss of Wilde's delicate aesthetic voice that defines this passage.

The phrase 'உலகறிந்த உண்மை' (universally known truth) is accurate, but the repetition of 'எண்ணங்கள் மற்றும் எண்ணங்கள்' (thoughts and thoughts) appears to be an error, and the overall structure feels more literal and less graceful than the source's sophisticated irony.

results.translated_text: என்னைக் கூப்பிடுங்கள் இஷ்மாவேல் என்று. சில வருடங்களுக்கு முன்பு—சரியாக எவ்வளவு காலம் என்று குறிப்பிடத் தேவையில்லை—என்னுடைய கையில் மிகக் குறைவான பணமே இருந்தது, மேலும் கரையில் எனக்குப் பெரிதாக எதிலும் ஆர்வம் இல்லை. அதனால், நான் சிறிது தூரம் பயணம் செய்து, உலகின் கடற்பகுதியைப் பார்க்க வேண்டும் என்று நினைத்தேன். இது நான் மன அழுத்தத்தைக் குறைத்து, உடல் ஆரோக்கியத்தை சீராக வைத்துக்கொள்ளும் ஒரு வழி. நான் எப்போது மனச்சோர்வு அடைகிறேனோ, அல்லது என் ஆன்மாவில் ஈரப்பதமான, வருத்தமான நவம்பர் மாதத்தைப் போல உணர்கிறேனோ, அப்போது உடனடியாகக் கடலுக்குச் செல்ல வேண்டிய நேரம் வந்துவிட்டது என்று நான் கருதுகிறேன்.

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core narrative and philosophical meaning reasonably well, but struggles with the archaic literary tone and metaphorical elegance of the original. The opening is slightly awkward ('என்னைக் கூப்பிடுங்கள் இஷ்மாவேல் என்று'), and the poetic melancholy of phrases like 'damp, drizzly November in my soul' loses its evocative power through literal translation, resulting in a more prosaic rendering that doesn't fully preserve Melville's distinctive voice.

results.translated_text: Всеобщее признание гласит, что одинокий мужчина, обладающий значительным состоянием, непременно должен стремиться к женитьбе. Независимо от того, насколько известны чувства и взгляды такого человека при его первом появлении в определенном районе, эта истина настолько глубоко укоренилась в умах окружающих семей, что он считается законной добычей одной из их дочерей.

scores.feedback: The translation accurately conveys the meaning and captures Austen's ironic tone through phrases like 'законной добычей' (lawful prey), though 'Всеобщее признание гласит' is slightly less elegant than the original's opening. Minor fluency issues with 'определенном районе' (specific district) being less precise than 'neighbourhood,' and the overall rhythm feels somewhat more formal and less naturally ironic than Austen's original prose.

results.translated_text: В мастерской царил насыщенный аромат роз, и когда легкий летний ветер колыхал деревья в саду, через открытую дверь доносился густой запах сирени или более нежный аромат розового шиповника. Лорд Генри Уоттон, лежа на диване, обитом персидским кожей, и, как обычно, затягиваясь бесчисленными сигаретами, из угла дивана мог едва различить отблеск медовых, золотистых цветков лаврки.

scores.feedback: The translation captures most of the meaning but contains notable errors: 'Persian saddle-bags' is mistranslated as 'Persian leather' (персидской кожей), and 'laburnum' becomes 'лаврки' (unclear/incorrect botanical term). The sensory richness is partially preserved but the prose feels somewhat stilted and less flowing than Wilde's original aesthetic style.

results.translated_text: "Вы, должно быть, побывали в Афганистане", - заметил он. "Откуда вы это узнали?" - спросил я, поражённый. "Неважно", - ответил он, тихо посмеиваясь. "Сейчас речь идет о гемоглобине. Неужели вы не видите, насколько важно мое открытие?" "Конечно, это интересно с химической точки зрения", - ответил я, "но с практической точки зрения..." "Послушайте, это самое важное медико-юридическое открытие за последние годы. Разве вы не видите, что это дает нам абсолютно надежный способ определения крови?"

scores.feedback: The translation accurately conveys the meaning and captures Holmes's deductive brilliance and Watson's astonishment, though some phrases like 'тихо посмеиваясь' (quietly chuckling) slightly soften the original tone, and 'Послушайте' (Listen) adds emphasis not present in 'Why, man' which is more colloquial. The dialogue flows naturally in Russian but with minor stylistic adjustments that slightly alter the Victorian-era character voice.

results.translated_text: Я отправился в лес, потому что хотел жить осознанно, соприкасаться только с самыми важными аспектами жизни и узнать, чему он может меня научить, чтобы, когда придет мой час, я не обнаружил, что не прожил свою жизнь. Я не хотел жить жизнью, которая была бы не настоящей, ведь жизнь так ценна; и я не хотел прибегать к смирению, если это было не абсолютно необходимо. Я хотел жить глубоко и извлечь из жизни всю ее суть.
